rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DINO TACCINI TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondents Notice Of Joint Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Petitioners ALDADINO DeRANIERI, MARCUS DeRANIERI, RENO DeRANIERI , and Respondent SACRED HEART CAHTEDRAL's Joint Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement is hereby tentatively granted. Within five (5) days of entry of the FINAL ORDER Respondent UGO shall comply with the terms of the Settlement Agreement and make the required distributions from THE TACCINI TRUSTS to (a) SHC in the amount of $72,333 (b) DeRANIERIS in the amount of $82,333.00 payable to ALADINO DeRANIERI in the amount of $27,444.34, to MARCUS DeRANIERI IN THE AMOUNT OF $27,444.33, and to RENO DeRANIERI in the amount of $27,444.33 (c) In addition Respondent UGO is to forego any further distribution to himself as beneficiary. If Respondent UGO fails to comply within five days of the entry of the Court's Final Order, then GREGORY WALKER is to be appointed as elisor to enforce the terms of the Settlement Agreement. The Court also hereby tentatively grants Petitioners DeRANIERI and Respondent SACRED HEARTH CATHEDRAL's motion for attorney's fees. Pursuant to NOL, Ex 1, par 20, the prevailing Party is entitled to reasonable attorneys' fees and costs. The Court is unable to make a determination of reasonable fees based on the Declaration of Gerald M. Murphy and Eli Coffino-Greenberg. The attorneys failed to attach time slips that show services provided and the time expanded. In order for the Court to make a determination of reasonable fees the attorneys need to file a declaration in support of attorneys' fees and attach their daily entries of time slip that show what services were provided and the time expanded. The amount of attorneys' fees can be mediated by the parties or resubmit to the court for determination. Any party who wishes to argue the motion must notify the court (415-551-3702) and opposing counsel no later than 5:00 p.m. on July 24, 2013. If the tentative ruling is not argued, it will become the order of the court. Prevailing party to prepare the order.
